channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: EU putting tariff countermeasures on hold for 90 days after Trump pause
item.description: The European Union (EU) is putting its retaliatory tariffs on hold for 90 days after President Trump instituted a pause on his sweeping duties for most U.S. trading partners, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen unveiled early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president targets China influence, kicks out pro-Beijing agitators amid rising tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President William Lai promises an uncompressing stance against both domestic and foreign pro-CCP influence operations amid rising tensions between Taipei and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ksenia Karelina, a US-Russia dual national, freed in a prisoner swap between Moscow and Washington
item.description: Russia has freed a Russian American convicted of treason in exchange for a Russian-German man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S. Thursday's prisoner swap comes as the two countries work to repair ties.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Zealand lawmakers reject proposed law to redefine the country's founding Treaty of Waitangi
item.description: New Zealand lawmakers have dealt an overwhelming defeat to a controversial proposed law seeking to redefine the country’s founding treaty between Māori tribes and the British Crown.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Defense chiefs from around 30 countries expected at NATO for talks on a future Ukraine force
item.description: Britain and France are convening a meeting of defense ministers from around 30 countries to press ahead with plans to deploy troops to Ukraine to police any future peace agreement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China reaches out to others as Trump layers on tariffs
item.description: China is reaching out to other nations as the U.S. layers on tariffs, in what appears to be an attempt by Beijing to form a united front to compel Washington to retreat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Number of dead in a roof collapse at a nightclub in Dominican Republic rises to 218
item.description: The number of dead in a roof collapse at an iconic nightclub in the Dominican Republic has surged to 218.
item.dc:creator: Martín Adames Alcántara And Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the Dominican capital collapsed during a merengue concert, killing at least 184 people and injuring over 200 more.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 50 years later, Vietnam's battlefields draw retrospective veterans and other tourists
item.description: HO CHI MINH CITY, Vietnam (AP) — Hamburger Hill, Hue, the Ia Drang Valley, Khe Sanh: Some remember the Vietnam War battles from the headlines of the 1960s and 1970s, others from movies and history books.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and HAU DINH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The United States and China are locked in a faceoff over tariffs. No one wants to blink first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The tariff fight between the world's two largest economies spiraled into greater peril Wednesday as President Donald Trump tried to narrow his global trade war into a direct — and risky — faceoff with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China accuses US of 'blackmail' after Hegseth's Panama Canal remarks
item.description: The Chinese government is accusing the U.S. of “blackmail” after U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared Beijing a threat to the Panama Canal, asserting that Washington along with Panama “will take back” the waterway from Chinese influence.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Trump restoring some foreign aid programs
item.description: The Trump administration on Tuesday claimed foreign aid programs that were cut in error would be restored.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US 'alarmed' after American academic charged with insulting monarchy in Thailand
item.description: American academic Paul Chambers is currently in custody in Thailand for allegedly insulting the country’s monarchy, a crime punishable by years in prison in the Southeast Asian country. State Department spokesperson Tammy Bruce said in a Tuesday statement that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Nicaragua’s crackdown on the Catholic Church is worsening, exiles and human rights advocates say
item.description: Preparations for popular, often daylong Lent and Holy Week processions are underway across Latin America — but not in Nicaragua. They’ve been largely banned for a second year, one of many concerns for the faithful in a country that human rights advocates, exiled priests and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's president again pledged Wednesday that his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States, going as far as dangling the prospect of direct American investment in the Islamic Republic if the countries can reach a deal.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korean leader's sister denounces denuclearization calls, saying nuclear status is permanent
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un on Wednesday mocked Washington and its Asian allies for what she called their “daydream” of denuclearizing the North, insisting that the country will never give up its nuclear weapons program.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Slain Hamas hostage's family fights for the release of those still in Gaza
item.description: Oded Lifshitz, an 83-year-old great-grandfather, returned to Israel in a coffin more than 500 days after he was taken from his home. Now, his family is fighting for the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's foreign minister says he will have indirect talks with US envoy over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran 's foreign minister said Tuesday he'll meet with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff in Oman for the first negotiations under the Trump administration seeking to halt Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program as tensions remain high in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indigenous march in Brazil to demand more land be set aside for their stewardship
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — With the first U.N. climate talks in the Amazon approaching, thousands of Indigenous people marched Tuesday in Brazil’s capital, demanding the state guarantee and expand their rights to traditional lands as part of the solution to the world’s climate crisis.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Chinese men captured in Ukraine, Zelenskyy claims
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for action in the U.S. and Europe after claiming to capture two Chinese nationals fighting in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 18 dead, 120 injured in Dominican Republican nightclub rooftop collapse
item.description: 18 people died and more than 120 were injured when a roof fell at a nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea says 10 North Korean soldiers crossed military demarcation line, warning shots fired
item.description: South Korea's military said troops aired warning broadcasts and fired warning shots after approximately 10 North Korean soldiers crossed the military demarcation line in the demilitarized zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu to meet at White House as Israel seeks tariff relief, discussions on Iran, Gaza hostages
item.description: President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu set for White House meeting to discuss ending U.S. tariffs on Israel, the war in Gaza, the release of hostages and Iran's nuclear threat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US revokes visas for South Sudanese as civil war looms at home
item.description: Here’s a look at South Sudan, whose people had been granted temporary protected status by the U.S. because of insecurity at home. That status expires on May 3.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas terror outlet quietly cuts Gaza death count, reveals most killed were combat-age men
item.description: New analysis shows thousands of names were removed from the Gaza casualty list as Hamas terror organization admits most deaths aged between 13 to 55 were males.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope appears in St. Peter's Square for first time in weeks
item.description: Pope Francis greeted crowds in St. Peter's Square on Sunday, his first public appearance since being discharged from the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: The Who... not too old to rock
item.description: Roger Daltrey and Pete Townshend lit up London in a rare, fiery performance for Teenage Cancer Trust — decades older, still unmistakably The Who.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: China slaps a 34% tax on all US imports in retaliation for Trump's tariffs
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tax on all U.S. imports next week, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new tariffs that delivered the strongest response yet from Beijing to the American leader's trade war.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, NewsNation
